audit_items.proof_trace: `rewrite_session_var_get` at L293 calls `strdup(var->lv_value.bv_val)` with no null check on `bv_val`; `bv_val` can become NULL when `rewrite_var_replace` (called at session.c:L233 via `(void)rewrite_var_replace(...)`) frees the old value then fails to strdup the new one under OOM, setting `bv_val = NULL` in the AVL tree entry and returning -1 — a return value that `rewrite_session_var_set_f` silently discards. A subsequent get on the same session variable then passes NULL to strdup, which is undefined behavior and a crash on all common platforms.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An LDAP client that triggers a rewrite rule using session variables (REWRITE_MAP_SET_SESN_VAR followed by REWRITE_MAP_GET_SESN_VAR on the same variable name) can reach this path: if server memory is exhausted at the moment a session variable is updated with a longer value, the old `bv_val` is freed and `strdup` returns NULL, corrupting the in-tree var; a follow-up GET request for the same session variable then calls `strdup(NULL)` in `rewrite_session_var_get::L293`, crashing slapd.
